root_text.an7.68:1.1: “Sattahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.68:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaññū ca hoti atthaññū ca attaññū ca mattaññū ca kālaññū ca parisaññū ca puggalaparoparaññū ca.
root_text.an7.68:2.3: suttaṁ geyyaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ gāthaṁ udānaṁ itivuttakaṁ jātakaṁ abbhutadhammaṁ vedallaṁ.
root_text.an7.68:2.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an7.68:2.5: suttaṁ geyyaṁ …pe… abbhutadhammaṁ vedallaṁ, nayidha ‘dhammaññū’ti vucceyya.
root_text.an7.68:2.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ jānāti—
root_text.an7.68:2.7: suttaṁ geyyaṁ …pe… abbhutadhammaṁ vedallaṁ, tasmā ‘dhammaññū’ti vuccati.
root_text.an7.68:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tassa tasseva bhāsitassa atthaṁ jānāti:
root_text.an7.68:3.3: ‘ayaṁ imassa bhāsitassa attho, ayaṁ imassa bhāsitassa attho’ti.
root_text.an7.68:3.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tassa tasseva bhāsitassa atthaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an7.68:3.5: ‘ayaṁ imassa bhāsitassa attho, ayaṁ imassa bhāsitassa attho’ti, nayidha ‘atthaññū’ti vucceyya.
root_text.an7.68:3.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tassa tasseva bhāsitassa atthaṁ jānāti:
root_text.an7.68:3.7: ‘ayaṁ imassa bhāsitassa attho, ayaṁ imassa bhāsitassa attho’ti, tasmā ‘atthaññū’ti vuccati.
root_text.an7.68:4.3: ‘ettakomhi saddhāya sīlena sutena cāgena paññāya paṭibhānenā’ti.
root_text.an7.68:4.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attānaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an7.68:4.5: ‘ettakomhi saddhāya sīlena sutena cāgena paññāya paṭibhānenā’ti, nayidha ‘attaññū’ti vucceyya.
root_text.an7.68:4.6: Yasmā ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attānaṁ jānāti:
root_text.an7.68:4.7: ‘ettakomhi saddhāya sīlena sutena cāgena paññāya paṭibhānenā’ti, tasmā ‘attaññū’ti vuccati.
root_text.an7.68:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu mattaṁ jānāti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ paṭiggahaṇāya.
root_text.an7.68:5.3: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu mattaṁ jāneyya cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ paṭiggahaṇāya, nayidha ‘mattaññū’ti vucceyya.
root_text.an7.68:5.4: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu mattaṁ jānāti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ paṭiggahaṇāya, tasmā ‘mattaññū’ti vuccati.
root_text.an7.68:6.3: ‘ayaṁ kālo uddesassa, ayaṁ kālo paripucchāya, ayaṁ kālo yogassa, ayaṁ kālo paṭisallānassā’ti.
root_text.an7.68:6.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an7.68:6.5: ‘ayaṁ kālo uddesassa, ayaṁ kālo paripucchāya, ayaṁ kālo yogassa, ayaṁ kālo paṭisallānassā’ti, nayidha ‘kālaññū’ti vucceyya.
root_text.an7.68:6.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālaṁ jānāti:
root_text.an7.68:6.7: ‘ayaṁ kālo uddesassa, ayaṁ kālo paripucchāya, ayaṁ kālo yogassa, ayaṁ kālo paṭisallānassā’ti, tasmā ‘kālaññū’ti vuccati.
root_text.an7.68:6.8: Iti dhammaññū, atthaññū, attaññū, mattaññū, kālaññū.
root_text.an7.68:7.3: ‘ayaṁ khattiyaparisā, ayaṁ brāhmaṇaparisā, ayaṁ gahapatiparisā, ayaṁ samaṇaparisā.
root_text.an7.68:7.4: Tattha evaṁ upasaṅkamitabbaṁ, evaṁ ṭhātabbaṁ, evaṁ kattabbaṁ, evaṁ nisīditabbaṁ, evaṁ bhāsitabbaṁ, evaṁ tuṇhī bhavitabban’ti.
root_text.an7.68:7.5: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu parisaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an7.68:7.8: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu parisaṁ jānāti:
root_text.an7.68:7.9: ‘ayaṁ khattiyaparisā, ayaṁ brāhmaṇaparisā, ayaṁ gahapatiparisā, ayaṁ samaṇaparisā.
root_text.an7.68:7.10: Tattha evaṁ upasaṅkamitabbaṁ, evaṁ ṭhātabbaṁ, evaṁ kattabbaṁ, evaṁ nisīditabbaṁ, evaṁ bhāsitabbaṁ, evaṁ tuṇhī bhavitabban’ti, tasmā ‘parisaññū’ti vuccati.
root_text.an7.68:7.11: Iti dhammaññū, atthaññū, attaññū, mattaññū, kālaññū, parisaññū.
root_text.an7.68:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno dvayena puggalā viditā honti.
root_text.an7.68:8.4: eko ariyānaṁ dassanakāmo, eko ariyānaṁ na dassanakāmo.
root_text.an7.68:9.2: eko saddhammaṁ sotukāmo, eko saddhammaṁ na sotukāmo.
root_text.an7.68:10.2: eko ohitasoto dhammaṁ suṇāti, eko anohitasoto dhammaṁ suṇāti.
root_text.an7.68:11.2: eko sutvā dhammaṁ dhāreti, eko sutvā dhammaṁ na dhāreti.
root_text.an7.68:11.3: Yvāyaṁ puggalo sutvā na dhammaṁ dhāreti,
root_text.an7.68:12.2: eko dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthaṁ upaparikkhati, eko dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthaṁ na upaparikkhati.
root_text.an7.68:12.3: Yvāyaṁ puggalo dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthaṁ na upaparikkhati,
root_text.an7.68:12.5: Yvāyaṁ puggalo dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthaṁ upaparikkhati,
root_text.an7.68:13.1: Dve puggalā dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthaṁ upaparikkhanti—
root_text.an7.68:13.2: eko atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno, eko atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya na dhammānudhammappaṭipanno.
root_text.an7.68:13.3: Yvāyaṁ puggalo atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya na dhammānudhammappaṭipanno,
root_text.an7.68:14.2: eko attahitāya paṭipanno no parahitāya, eko attahitāya ca paṭipanno parahitāya ca.
root_text.an7.68:14.3: Yvāyaṁ puggalo attahitāya paṭipanno no parahitāya,
root_text.an7.68:14.5: Yvāyaṁ puggalo attahitāya ca paṭipanno parahitāya ca,
root_text.an7.68:15.1: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno dvayena puggalā viditā honti.
root_text.an7.68:16.2: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an7.68:1.1: “A mendicant with seven qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an7.68:1.3: It’s when a mendicant knows the teachings, knows the meaning, has self-knowledge, knows moderation, knows the right time, knows assemblies, and knows individuals high and low.
translation_text.an7.68:2.1: And how is a mendicant one who knows the teachings?
translation_text.an7.68:2.2: It’s when a mendicant knows the teachings:
translation_text.an7.68:2.3: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an7.68:2.4: If a mendicant did not know these teachings,
translation_text.an7.68:2.5: they would not be called ‘one who knows the teachings’.
translation_text.an7.68:2.6: But because they do know these teachings,
translation_text.an7.68:2.7: they are called ‘one who knows the teachings’.
translation_text.an7.68:2.8: Such is the one who knows the teachings.
translation_text.an7.68:3.1: And how are they one who knows the meaning?
translation_text.an7.68:3.2: It’s when a mendicant knows the meaning of this or that statement:
translation_text.an7.68:3.3: ‘This is what that statement means; that is what this statement means.’
translation_text.an7.68:3.4: If a mendicant did not know the meaning of this or that statement,
translation_text.an7.68:3.5: they would not be called ‘one who knows the meaning’.
translation_text.an7.68:3.6: But because they do know the meaning of this or that statement,
translation_text.an7.68:3.7: they are called ‘one who knows the meaning’.
translation_text.an7.68:3.8: Such is the one who knows the teachings and the one who knows the meaning.
translation_text.an7.68:4.1: And how are they one who has self-knowledge?
translation_text.an7.68:4.2: It’s when a mendicant has self-knowledge:
translation_text.an7.68:4.3: ‘This is the extent of my faith, ethics, learning, generosity, wisdom, and eloquence.’
translation_text.an7.68:4.4: If a mendicant did not have self-knowledge,
translation_text.an7.68:4.5: they would not be called ‘one who has self-knowledge’.
translation_text.an7.68:4.6: But because they do have self-knowledge,
translation_text.an7.68:4.7: they are called ‘one who has self-knowledge’.
translation_text.an7.68:4.8: Such is the one who knows the teachings, the one who knows the meaning, and the one who has self-knowledge.
translation_text.an7.68:5.1: And how are they one who knows moderation?
translation_text.an7.68:5.2: It’s when a mendicant knows moderation when receiving robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an7.68:5.3: If a mendicant did not know moderation, they would not be called ‘one who knows moderation’.
translation_text.an7.68:5.4: But because they do know moderation, they are called ‘one who knows moderation’.
translation_text.an7.68:5.5: Such is the one who knows the teachings, the one who knows the meaning, the one who has self-knowledge, and the one who knows moderation.
translation_text.an7.68:6.1: And how are they one who knows the right time?
translation_text.an7.68:6.2: It’s when a mendicant knows the right time:
translation_text.an7.68:6.3: ‘This is the time for recitation; this is the time for questioning; this is the time for meditation; this is the time for retreat.’
translation_text.an7.68:6.4: If a mendicant did not know the right time,
translation_text.an7.68:6.5: they would not be called ‘one who knows the right time’.
translation_text.an7.68:6.6: But because they do know the right time,
translation_text.an7.68:6.7: they are called ‘one who knows the right time’.
translation_text.an7.68:6.8: Such is the one who knows the teachings, the one who knows the meaning, the one who has self-knowledge, the one who knows moderation, and the one who knows the right time.
translation_text.an7.68:7.1: And how are they one who knows assemblies?
translation_text.an7.68:7.2: It’s when a mendicant knows assemblies:
translation_text.an7.68:7.3: ‘This is an assembly of aristocrats, of brahmins, of householders, or of ascetics.
translation_text.an7.68:7.4: This one should be approached in this way. This is how to stand, to act, to sit, to speak, or to stay silent when there.’
translation_text.an7.68:7.5: If a mendicant did not know assemblies,
translation_text.an7.68:7.7: they would not be called ‘one who knows assemblies’.
translation_text.an7.68:7.8: But because they do know assemblies,
translation_text.an7.68:7.10: they are called ‘one who knows assemblies’.
translation_text.an7.68:7.11: Such is the one who knows the teachings, the one who knows the meaning, the one who has self-knowledge, the one who knows moderation, the one who knows the right time, and the one who knows assemblies.
translation_text.an7.68:8.1: And how are they one who knows individuals high and low?
translation_text.an7.68:8.2: It’s when a mendicant understands individuals in terms of pairs.
translation_text.an7.68:8.4: one likes to see the noble ones, one does not.
translation_text.an7.68:8.5: The individual who doesn’t like to see the noble ones
translation_text.an7.68:8.7: The individual who does like to see the noble ones
translation_text.an7.68:9.1: Two individuals like to see the noble ones:
translation_text.an7.68:9.2: one likes to hear the true teaching, one does not.
translation_text.an7.68:9.3: The individual who doesn’t like to hear the true teaching
translation_text.an7.68:9.5: The individual who does like to hear the true teaching
translation_text.an7.68:10.1: Two individuals like to hear the true teaching:
translation_text.an7.68:10.2: one actively listens to the teaching, one does not.
translation_text.an7.68:10.3: The individual who doesn’t actively listen to the teaching
translation_text.an7.68:10.5: The individual who does actively listen to the teaching
translation_text.an7.68:11.1: Two individuals actively listen to the teaching:
translation_text.an7.68:11.2: one remembers the teaching they’ve heard, one does not.
translation_text.an7.68:11.3: The individual who doesn’t remember the teaching they’ve heard
translation_text.an7.68:11.5: The individual who does remember the teaching they’ve heard
translation_text.an7.68:12.1: Two individuals remember the teaching they’ve heard:
translation_text.an7.68:12.2: one examines the meaning of the teachings they have remembered, one does not.
translation_text.an7.68:12.3: The individual who does not examine the meaning of the teachings they have remembered
translation_text.an7.68:12.5: The individual who does examine the meaning of the teachings they have remembered
translation_text.an7.68:13.1: Two individuals examine the meaning of the teachings they have remembered:
translation_text.an7.68:13.2: one understands the meaning and the teaching and practicesin line with the teaching, one understands the meaning and the teaching but does not practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an7.68:13.3: The individual who understands the meaning and the teaching but does not practice in line with the principle of the teaching
translation_text.an7.68:13.5: The individual who understands the meaning and the teaching and practices in line with the principle of the teaching
translation_text.an7.68:14.1: Two individuals understand the meaning and the teaching and practice in line with the teaching:
translation_text.an7.68:14.2: one practices to benefit themselves but not others, and one practices to benefit both themselves and others.
translation_text.an7.68:14.3: The individual who practices to benefit themselves but not others
translation_text.an7.68:14.5: The individual who practices to benefit both themselves and others
translation_text.an7.68:15.1: That’s how a mendicant understands individuals in terms of pairs.
translation_text.an7.68:16.1: That’s how a mendicant is one who knows individuals high and low.
translation_text.an7.68:16.2: A mendicant with these seven qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.”
